clusters.title: Boy, 16, arrested after police discover body of 13-year-old girl in Victorian bushland
clusters.neutral_headline: Teenager arrested after remains found during search for missing 13-year-old girl
bullet_summary: Detectives are investigating after human remains were found during a search for a missing teenage girl in Victoria's west
Reported by Guardian Australia, ABC News Australia

items.title: Boy, 16, arrested after police discover body of 13-year-old girl in Victorian bushland
items.standfirst: Police say death of Layla Jeffery ‘suspicious’ after remains found in town of Donald in western VictoriaGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA 16-year-old boy has been arrested after the body of a 13-year-old girl, who went missing on the weekend, was found in bushland in regional Victoria.Victoria police said they were treating Layla Jeffery’s death as “suspicious” after her remains were found in Donald, a small town in western Victoria, late on Thursday afternoon following an extensive search. Continue reading...
items.content: Police say death of Layla Jeffery ‘suspicious’ after remains found in town of Donald in western VictoriaGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA 16-year-old boy has been arrested after the body of a 13-year-old girl, who went missing on the weekend, was found in bushland in regional Victoria.Victoria police said they were treating Layla Jeffery’s death as “suspicious” after her remains were found in Donald, a small town in western Victoria, late on Thursday afternoon following an extensive search. Continue reading...

items.title: Teenager arrested after remains found during search for missing 13-year-old girl

items.title: First native bird found with H5 bird flu, marking 'devastating' new reality
items.standfirst: CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the detection of H5 bird flu in a greater crested tern — the first time the strain has been detected in an Australian seabird, after previous confirmed cases involved migratory birds from the sub-Antarctic region.
items.content: CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the detection of H5 bird flu in a greater crested tern — the first time the strain has been detected in an Australian seabird, after previous confirmed cases involved migratory birds from the sub-Antarctic region.

clusters.title: This Place: Boonoo Moodla
clusters.neutral_headline: This Place: Boonoo Moodla

items.title: This Place: Boonoo Moodla
items.standfirst: Boonoo Moodla, Billy Lights Point, has been a place of gathering and fishing for the Barngarla people for thousands of years. As part of NAIDOC Week the ABC is looking at Indigenous peoples' connection to country, and what makes it so special. NAIDOC Week is marking 50 years of Deadly and runs from July 5 until July 12.
items.content: Boonoo Moodla, Billy Lights Point, has been a place of gathering and fishing for the Barngarla people for thousands of years. As part of NAIDOC Week the ABC is looking at Indigenous peoples' connection to country, and what makes it so special. NAIDOC Week is marking 50 years of Deadly and runs from July 5 until July 12.
